# UC-13.7.1 · Fluentd Buffer Overflow and Retry Exhaustion Events

## Description

When Fluentd cannot flush faster than inputs arrive, chunks block and events are dropped or stalled—often the first sign of downstream indexer saturation. Tracking overflow and retry-exhaustion strings prioritizes the correct output plugin to fix.

## Value

Prevents silent log loss during traffic spikes and gives SREs a faster cue than disk-full pages alone.

## Implementation

Tag `plugin_id` from structured logs when possible. Correlate with Splunk indexer queue KPIs. Tune `retry_forever` and secondary destinations per vendor guidance.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:log"
| search "buffer" AND ("overflow" OR "chunk full" OR "retry limit" OR "giving up" OR "emit error")
| stats count, latest(_time) as t, values(plugin_id) as plugins by host, buffer_path
| where count >= 5
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Event timeline, bar chart (host × plugin), single-value (open buffer faults).

## References

- [Fluentd — Buffering](https://docs.fluentd.org/buffer)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
